title: Supabase Mobile Integration Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Supabase setup, authentication, real-time subscriptions, and Row Level Security.

questions.question: Why is `detectSessionInUrl: false` required in the Supabase client configuration for React Native?

options.text: React Native uses a different authentication protocol than web browsers
React Native has no window.location object — session tokens are passed via deep link callbacks, not URL parameters the SDK can auto-detect
It improves performance by skipping URL parsing on every API call
It is required to enable AsyncStorage as the auth storage backend

questions.question: What is the purpose of Row Level Security (RLS) in Supabase when accessed directly from a mobile app?

options.text: It compresses database rows to reduce network bandwidth
It enforces authorization rules at the database level so each user can only access their own rows — replacing the need for a backend API to enforce ownership
It limits the number of rows returned per query to prevent memory issues on mobile
It automatically encrypts sensitive columns like passwords and email addresses

questions.question: In Supabase real-time subscriptions, what underlying mechanism does Postgres use to push changes to connected clients?

options.text: HTTP long-polling — Supabase polls the database every 100ms for changes
PostgreSQL's LISTEN/NOTIFY mechanism broadcasts changes from the database to connected WebSocket clients via the Supabase Realtime server
GraphQL subscriptions — Supabase converts all real-time events to GraphQL format
Server-Sent Events — the database sends one-way change events via HTTP/2

questions.question: Which Supabase API key should be used in a mobile app, and why?

options.text: The service role key — it has full access to bypass RLS policies
The anon (public) key — it obeys RLS policies and is safe to expose; the service role key would bypass all security policies if embedded in the app
A custom generated key from the Supabase dashboard's mobile section
Either key is safe since both are encrypted in transit

questions.question: What does `.select().single()` do after a Supabase `.insert()` call?

options.text: It fetches all rows from the table after the insert completes
It returns the newly inserted row as a single object instead of an array, and throws if zero or multiple rows are returned
It locks the row so it cannot be updated by other concurrent requests
It validates that the inserted data matches the table schema before committing
